summary: Age appears to reduce personality disorder severity over time due to lower rates among older individuals and improved outcomes when disorders present later in life. This reduction likely influences associated maladaptive coping mechanisms, suggesting age moderately affects how personality disorders manifest through coping behaviors in psychiatric patients.
